mission: Build BrandBuild into an elite creative production console that lets operators combine Sora, Kling, and Higgsfield into a stronger end-to-end video workflow than any single engine alone.
primary_goals: Keep brandbuild.online stable and trustworthy in production.
Unify prompting, asset upload, generation orchestration, review, versioning, and export inside one premium dashboard.
Validate and harden live provider integrations for Sora, Kling, and Higgsfield.
Make the product feel polished enough to present as a serious venture-backed SaaS platform.
secondary_goals: Deepen the in-portal Studio Agent so it can guide project setup and provider routing.
Surface integration readiness, failures, and manual setup requirements clearly inside the product.
Keep durable project memory current so future sessions can reconstruct state from files alone.
non_goals: Do not build a full browser-based nonlinear editor in v1.
Do not expose provider secrets client-side.
Do not present mocked integrations as fully validated production features.
Do not reintroduce unrelated legacy product surfaces into BrandBuild.
hard_rules: Use BrandBuild as the user-facing product identity on production surfaces.
Keep provider API access behind the internal adapter layer.
Validate uploads for mime type and size and keep stored assets private.
Do not let important session context live only in chat; write it into repo-backed memory files.
Prefer production-minded vertical slices over disconnected prototypes.
Do not silently discard historical project memory; mark superseded or archived states clearly.
strong_preferences: Desktop-first workflow with strong operator guidance.
In-product visibility into system status, blockers, and next actions.
optional_preferences: Reduced-motion fallbacks for richer media surfaces.
Incremental expansion of telemetry, usage, and cost tracking.
business_constraints: BrandBuild is positioned as a high-end creative production operating system, not a generic multi-tool wrapper.
The strongest near-term value is seamless orchestration and operator efficiency, not breadth of features for their own sake.
technical_constraints: Current runtime stack is Next.js App Router + TypeScript + Supabase + Vercel.
Provider readiness depends on real account access beyond the presence of API keys.
The root package naming still uses ai-video-studio even though the product brand is BrandBuild.
compliance_notes: Uploaded customer assets and generated outputs should remain private by default.
Branded authentication email flows should hide Supabase as much as practical while keeping Supabase as the backend.
definitions_of_done: Production routes render without crashes on brandbuild.online.
Core workflow steps are understandable for a signed-in operator.
At least one real validated run exists for each provider integration targeted as live-capable.
Project memory files reflect the current product truth, blockers, and next steps.
